---
title: "Basket Grass (Oplismenus hirtellus)"
slug: "oplismenus-hirtellus"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/354675437/medium.jpeg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Basket Grass"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Poaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Poaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/354675437/medium.jpeg" alt="Basket Grass" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/lmcconachie">
lmcconachie</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/200968566">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Poaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=165865)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in shaded, moist habitats.

## Natural history notes

A low-growing, spreading grass characterized by its broad leaves and delicate, branching seed heads. In Florida, it is commonly found in shaded, moist habitats such as forests, hammocks, and along trails. It grows during the warmer months and can form dense patches on the forest floor, sometimes spreading aggressively in suitable conditions.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/165865-Oplismenus-hirtellus)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3707)
